card.empty.competitions: No competitions yet — create one in about two minutes.
card.empty.divisions: No divisions yet. A division picks the sport, its variant and format — entrants and fixtures live inside it.
confirm.downgrade.title: Downgrade to Community?
confirm.downgrade.body: Pro features become unavailable immediately. Anything over the Community limits is frozen, not deleted.
confirm.downgrade.label: Downgrade
confirm.leaveOrg.title: Leave this organisation?
confirm.leaveOrg.body: You lose access to {name} immediately. An owner can invite you back.
confirm.leaveOrg.label: Leave organisation
confirm.transferOwner.title: Transfer ownership?
confirm.transferOwner.body: You become an admin and cannot undo this yourself — only the new owner can transfer it back.
confirm.transferOwner.label: Transfer ownership
confirm.restoreCheckpoint.title: Restore this save point?
confirm.restoreCheckpoint.body: Schedule edits made after {name} are undone. Results are never touched.
confirm.restoreCheckpoint.label: Restore
confirm.clearSlots.title: Clear unlocked slots?
confirm.clearSlots.body: Every unlocked timetable slot in this division is cleared. Locked slots and results stay.
confirm.clearSlots.label: Clear slots
confirm.deleteClub.title: Delete this club?
confirm.deleteClub.body: {name} is removed. Its teams stay — their badges fall back to no club badge.
confirm.deleteClub.label: Delete club
confirm.withdrawRegistration.title: Withdraw this registration?
confirm.withdrawRegistration.body: The entrant comes off the list and their spot frees up.
confirm.withdrawRegistration.label: Withdraw
confirm.refundRegistration.title: Refund this registration?
confirm.refundRegistration.body: The remaining amount is refunded to the payer.
confirm.refundRegistration.label: Refund
confirm.markPaidRegistration.title: Mark this entry as paid?
confirm.markPaidRegistration.body: Records that you received the fee outside the app (cash or bank transfer) and confirms the entry. This is logged.
confirm.markPaidRegistration.label: Mark paid & confirm
confirm.waiveRegistration.title: Confirm without payment?
confirm.waiveRegistration.body: The entry is confirmed with the fee waived (comped). This is logged.
confirm.waiveRegistration.label: Waive fee & confirm
confirm.withdrawOwnRegistration.title: Withdraw your registration?
confirm.withdrawOwnRegistration.body: This frees your spot. You can register again while registration is open.
confirm.withdrawOwnRegistration.label: Withdraw
confirm.revokeKey.title: Revoke this API key?
confirm.revokeKey.body: Integrations using {name} stop working immediately. This cannot be undone.
confirm.revokeKey.label: Revoke key
confirm.deleteStage.title: Delete this stage?
confirm.deleteStage.body: {name} and its fixtures are removed. Completed results in other stages stay.
confirm.deleteStage.label: Delete stage
visibility.private.label: Private
visibility.private.consequence: Only your team can see it.
visibility.unlisted.label: Link only
visibility.unlisted.consequence: Anyone with the link can view. Hidden from Google and our directory.
visibility.public.label: Public
visibility.public.consequence: Anyone can find it — Google, and the Seazn discover page.
visibility.share.label: Share link
visibility.youth.title: This competition includes youth divisions
visibility.youth.body: Making it visible publishes players' names on the public page. Confirm you hold guardian consent for every under-age player before continuing.
confirm.withdrawEntrant.title: Withdraw {name}?
confirm.withdrawEntrant.body: In a running table stage: if they've played less than half their games, everything they played is voided (standings read as if they never entered); otherwise their results stand and remaining games walk over to opponents. In a bracket, opponents advance. This settles matches through the normal scoring ledger, so each one can be undone.
confirm.withdrawEntrant.label: Withdraw entrant
confirm.shiftAll.title: Shift every fixture by {minutes} minutes?
confirm.shiftAll.body: Locked and decided fixtures stay put. If the shift lands anything in a conflict, the board flags it — you can fix those matches individually, or undo the whole shift from the history panel.
confirm.shiftAll.label: Shift everything
board.stale: Schedule changed by someone else — board refreshed.
schedule.tz.caption: Times shown in {tz}
schedule.unscheduled.title: Not scheduled yet
register.full.title: This division is full
register.closed.title: Registration is closed
register.payments.unavailable.title: Card payments are temporarily unavailable
register.payments.unavailable.body: Try again shortly, or contact the organiser.
register.waitlist.noPayment: Full — join the waitlist, pay only if promoted.
register.ticket.title: You're registered
register.ticket.keep: Keep this reference — it's how you check your status and how the organiser finds you on the day.
register.guardian.title: Under-18 entry — guardian consent
register.consent.data: I agree that {org} and Seazn Club will store and process the details on this form (name, email address, date of birth) to run this competition.
showcase.label: Showcase on seazn.club
showcase.consent: By turning this on, this competition's name, your organisation's name, its sport(s), dates, location (if given), live scores and standings may appear on seazn.club's home, discovery and sport pages, and in marketing/social material. Requires public visibility — it switches off automatically if visibility drops. You can opt out any time; past marketing use is permitted, no new use after opt-out.
showcase.needsPublic: Set visibility to public to enable showcasing.
apiKeys.scope.read.label: Read
apiKeys.scope.read.line: Fetch competitions, fixtures, standings and registrations. Can't change anything.
apiKeys.scope.score.label: Score
apiKeys.scope.score.line: Everything Read does, plus push live scores and start divisions — for scoreboard integrations.
apiKeys.scope.manage.label: Manage
apiKeys.scope.manage.line: Full access: create competitions, generate fixtures, moderate registrations. Treat like a password.
apiKeys.pin.label: Limit to one competition
apiKeys.pin.line: A limited key only works inside the chosen competition — hand it to a vendor without exposing the rest.
apiKeys.manage.nudge: This key has full access — consider replacing it with a narrower one.
embed.title: Embed on your website
embed.line: Paste this snippet into your club site — it stays in sync by itself.
sponsors.title: Sponsors
sponsors.line: Sponsor logos appear on your public pages, the registration page and the TV slideshow.
claim.invite.tip: Email this player a link to take over their profile — see their matches, RSVP availability, manage their own consent.
claim.invite.title: Invite {name} to claim their profile
claim.invite.explain: They get an email with a claim link — only an account signed in with that address can accept it. Claiming lets them see their matches, RSVP availability and manage what's public.
claim.invite.sendFailed: The email couldn't be sent — copy the link below and share it with them yourself.
claim.invite.linkNote: The link works for 14 days and is only shown here once. Whoever opens it must sign in as the invited address.
claim.revoke.tip: Cancel the pending invite — the emailed link stops working immediately.
claim.unlink.tip: Disconnect the player's account from this profile. Rosters and results stay; you can invite them again later.
persons.merge.tip: Fold a duplicate entry into the player you keep. Can't be undone.
confirm.unlinkPlayer.title: Unlink this player account?
confirm.unlinkPlayer.body: {name} loses access to this profile on their player home. Matches and results stay. You can invite them to claim again later.
confirm.unlinkPlayer.label: Unlink
me.title: My matches & teams
me.claimed: Profile claimed — this is your player home now.
me.empty: Nothing here yet. When an organiser rosters you into a team or draw, your matches land on this page.
me.next.title: Next match
me.upcoming.title: Coming up
me.results.title: Recent results
me.teams.title: My teams
me.rsvp.notePlaceholder: Add a note for the organiser (optional)
me.consent.title: What's public about you
me.consent.line: These switches control whether your name and photo appear on public pages — per club.
me.consent.name: Show my name publicly
me.consent.locked: An organiser manages this until you're 16.
checkin.title: Match check-in
checkin.done.title: You're checked in
checkin.done.line: The organiser can see you're here. Good luck!
checkin.needsClaim.title: Claim your profile first
checkin.needsClaim.line: Check-in works once you've claimed your player profile. Ask the organiser to send you a claim invite, then scan again.
org.home.title: Competitions
org.home.subtitle.editor: A competition holds one or more divisions — each with its own sport, entrants and format.
org.home.subtitle.viewer: You have view-only access to this board.
aria.viewPublicNewTab: View Public Page (opens in a new tab)
aria.qrPoster: QR poster (PDF, opens in a new tab)
div.detail.standings.empty: No table stages in this division — standings apply to league, group and swiss stages.
div.detail.tiebreak.label: Tie-break cascade:
div.detail.embed.private: This competition is private, so there is nothing to embed. Set it to unlisted or public in competition settings first.
comp.settings.title: Settings — {name}
comp.schedule.title: Competition schedule — {name}
comp.schedule.empty: No divisions yet — the schedule board fills in once this competition has divisions with fixtures.
payments.title: Payments
payments.desc: How your entry fees are collected — card payments via Stripe, or your own cash / bank-transfer instructions. Each division picks its method in its registration settings.
settings.nav.title: Settings
settings.api.noAccess: Only owners and admins can manage API keys.
settings.emailChange.expired: This confirmation link has expired. Please request a new one.
settings.emailChange.taken: That email address is already in use by another account.
settings.emailChange.error: Something went wrong. Please try again.
settings.account.exportDesc: Download a copy of your profile, organizations, and tournaments.
settings.account.privacyDesc: Change or withdraw your consent for PostHog product analytics. See our
settings.account.soleOwner: Sole owner — invite others before you can transfer or leave.
settings.account.deleteDesc: Deleting your account removes you from all organizations and schedules your data for permanent erasure within 30 days.
billing.title: Plan & Billing
billing.checkoutComplete: Checkout complete — your plan is now
billing.credit: {amount} account credit — pays future invoices automatically.
billing.cardOnFile: Card on file — Pro continues after the trial.
billing.paymentFailed: Your last payment failed. Fix the card below, then retry.
billing.trialCopy.available: 14-day free trial · no card required · cancel anytime
billing.trialCopy.used: Billed from day one — your free trial has already been used · cancel anytime
upgrade.includes.formats: Advanced formats — double elimination, ladders, americano
upgrade.includes.fees: Online entry fees via Stripe (5% platform fee)
upgrade.includes.branding: Custom branding, PDF/XLSX exports, realtime slideshow
upgrade.title: Upgrade “{name}”
upgrade.active.title: Event Pass active ✓
upgrade.active.body: This competition is upgraded for its lifetime — divisions, entrants, formats, fees, branding and exports are all unlocked here.
upgrade.pro.title: You’re on Pro
upgrade.pro.body: Pro already covers everything an Event Pass adds — across every competition in your organization.
upgrade.intro: One payment upgrades this competition for its lifetime — no subscription, and it survives forever even if you never go Pro.
upgrade.ownerOnly: Only the organization owner can purchase upgrades.
upgrade.proCard.title: Running events all year?
upgrade.proCard.body: Pro upgrades every competition in your organization — unlimited events and divisions, 256 entrants per division, player stats, officials, API access and a 2% platform fee.
me.growth.q: Run your own league or tournament?
checkin.subtitle: Tell the organiser you’ve arrived — one tap.
checkin.signInPrompt: Sign in to check in — no password needed.
register.title: Register
register.subtitle: Pick a division, fill in your details — takes under a minute.
register.notOpen: Registration is not open for this competition.
status.pending.title: Registration received
status.pending.body: Your spot is held. If an entry fee is due, settle it below and the organiser confirms your entry.
status.pending_card.title: Almost in — payment confirms your spot
status.pending_card.body: Your spot is held until the deadline below. Pay by card and you’re confirmed instantly.
status.paid.title: Payment received
status.paid.body: Your payment is in — confirmation is being finalised.
status.confirmed.title: You’re in!
status.confirmed.body: Your registration is confirmed and you’re on the entrant list.
status.waitlisted.title: You’re on the waitlist
status.waitlisted.body: The division is full — no payment is taken while you wait. If a spot opens you’re promoted automatically; keep this page’s link.
status.withdrawn.title: Registration withdrawn
status.expired.title: Pay window passed
status.expired.body: This registration expired unpaid and the spot was released. If places remain you can register again.
status.waitlistPosition: You’re #{n} in line — we’ll email you if a spot opens.
status.howToPay: How to pay your entry fee
status.contactYou: The organiser will contact you at your registered email with payment details.
status.confirmsOnce: The organiser confirms your entry once the payment arrives.
status.cardUnavailable: Card payments are temporarily unavailable — try again shortly or contact the organiser.
status.keepLink: Keep this page’s link — it’s your receipt and the only way to manage this registration.
ref.line.paid: Payment received — confirmation is being finalised.
ref.line.waitlisted: On the waitlist — promoted automatically if a spot opens.
ref.line.expired: The pay window passed — this registration expired. Register again if spots remain.
ref.shareText: I’m registered for {division} at {competition} (ref {ref}). Follow it live:
register.ticket.legacyRef: Issued before reference numbers — your email link is your receipt.
import.title: Import participants
import.desc.pre: Bring clubs, teams and players in from one spreadsheet. Nothing is written until you commit the previewed plan. Divisions must
import.desc.post: — a Division column places each team as an entrant.
directory.title: Directory
directory.desc: Your organisation’s players and clubs — reused across every competition.
directory.players.desc: Players rostered into entrants across competitions. Date of birth is used only for eligibility and is never shown publicly; names appear on public pages only with consent. Spot the same person twice? Use
directory.clubs.desc: Parent clubs group teams across age groups and divisions. A club’s badge and colours cascade to every team that doesn’t set its own.
tips.division.visibility.title: Who can see a division
tips.division.visibility.body: A division follows its competition: Private is team-only, Link only means anyone with the link, Public is findable on Google and our discover page.
tips.division.start-locks.title: Starting locks the setup
tips.division.start-locks.body: Once scoring starts, entrants and format are locked so results stay fair. Finish seeding and structure first.
tips.scoring.seq-conflict.title: Someone else scored first
tips.scoring.seq-conflict.body: Two scorers updated the same match at once. The app refreshed to the latest score — re-enter your point if it's still missing.
tips.entrants.kinds.title: Teams, individuals and pairs
tips.entrants.kinds.body: A division registers one kind of entrant: whole teams, individual players, or fixed pairs (doubles). Pick the kind that matches who plays a fixture.
tips.persons.merge.title: Merging duplicate players
tips.persons.merge.body: Same person listed twice? Merge moves their team memberships, lineups and profiles onto the player you keep, then removes the duplicate. Results are untouched — but a merge can't be undone.
tips.persons.actions.title: Player actions
tips.persons.actions.body: Invite to claim emails the player a link to run their own profile — matches, RSVPs, consent. Unlink disconnects a claimed account; rosters and results stay. Merge… folds a duplicate into the player you keep.
tips.formats.picker.title: Choosing a format
tips.formats.picker.body: League plays everyone once and ranks by points. Knockout eliminates losers. Groups + knockout qualifies the top of each group. Swiss pairs equals with equals — good for big fields on short time.
tips.billing.downgrade-freeze.title: What downgrading freezes
tips.billing.downgrade-freeze.body: Nothing is deleted. Anything over the Community limits becomes read-only until you upgrade again or archive something.
tips.billing.event-pass.title: What an Event Pass covers
tips.billing.event-pass.body: A pass upgrades one competition for its lifetime — every division inside it gets Pro features. It doesn't carry to next season's edition.
tips.registration.ref-number.title: Reference numbers
tips.registration.ref-number.body: Every registration gets a short reference like R-7F3K. Players use it to find, pay for or withdraw their entry — no account needed.
tips.schedule.locking.title: Pinned slots
tips.schedule.locking.body: Pin a match (📌 → 🔒) and the auto passes — Auto-schedule, Re-flow remaining, Clear slots — leave it exactly where it is. You can still drag or move a pinned match yourself, and it stays pinned at its new slot. To stop ALL edits, freeze the whole schedule from the History panel instead.
tips.schedule.undo-watermark.title: Undo and save points
tips.schedule.undo-watermark.body: Every schedule change is undoable, and a save point marks a known-good timetable you can restore. Match results are never touched by either.
tips.api.key-scopes.title: Key scopes
tips.api.key-scopes.body: Read keys only fetch data. Score keys can also push live scores. Manage keys can change anything — treat them like passwords and revoke any key you no longer use.
tips.slideshow.url.title: The slideshow URL
tips.slideshow.url.body: Open it on any TV or projector browser — it cycles standings and matchups by itself and keeps itself up to date.
tips.register.youth.title: Why guardian consent?
tips.register.youth.body: This division is for under-18 players, so a parent or guardian confirms the entry. Public pages show shortened names for youth divisions.
tips.board.filter.title: Filter by division
tips.board.filter.body: These chips are the divisions on this board. Tap any of them to show just those divisions; tap again to bring one back. The filter lives in the page URL, so you can share or bookmark a filtered view.
tips.schedule.save-points.title: Save points
tips.schedule.save-points.body: A save point bookmarks the timetable exactly as it is now — every kick-off time and court. Restore rewinds the schedule to that bookmark by undoing each change since, one by one. Match results are never touched: if rewinding would erase a played result, the restore stops there. One save point is free; more need Pro.
persons.mergingPost: into… pick the player to keep below.
persons.consent.name: Name
persons.consent.shownTip: {label} is shown on public pages — click to hide
persons.consent.hiddenTip: {label} is hidden from public pages — click to show

clubs.empty: No clubs yet — add one above or bring them in with the spreadsheet import.
clubs.detail.title: {name} — teams across divisions
clubs.detail.noTeams: No teams belong to this club yet.
clubs.team.name: Team name
clubs.squad.empty: No players in this squad yet.
clubs.squad.noPlayers: No players yet — add them under Players.
clubs.logo.title: Bulk logo upload
clubs.logo.descPre: Drop image files named after clubs (e.g.
clubs.logo.descPost: ) — every team in a club inherits its badge. Unmatched files can be re-mapped below.
clubs.logo.assignRemaining: Assign remaining files to clubs without a badge, in order
auth.emailRequired: Enter your email to get a sign-in link.
auth.check.title: Check your email
auth.check.bodyPre: We sent a sign-in link to
auth.check.bodyPost: . Open it to sign in — it expires in 15 minutes.
auth.newHint: No password needed. New here? Entering your email creates your account.
login.subtitle: Organise chess, carrom, cricket and more — across your community.
join.invalidLink: This invite link is not valid.
join.title: Join {org}
join.role.admin: Admins can create and manage tournaments, results and members.
join.role.viewer: Viewers get read-only access to the board.
join.role.owner: Owners have full control of the organization.
join.role.scorer: Scorers record results for the matches assigned to them.
join.emailMismatch: This is a personal invite for {inviteEmail}, but you’re signed in as {userEmail}. Sign in with the invited address, or ask the organiser to invite this one.
join.member.additive: You are already a {role} of this organization — accepting adds the invited matches to your scoring assignments. Your current access is unchanged.
join.member.noChange: You are already a {role} of this organization — accepting changes nothing.
join.signInPrompt: Sign in or create an account to join.
onboarding.subtitle: Create a competition, add a division for your sport, register entrants — and you’re scoring within minutes.
orgNew.title: Create an organization
orgNew.subtitle: An organization is your board — it holds your seasons, tournaments and team members.
orgNew.renameHint: You can rename it later in Settings.
verifyEmail.confirming: Confirming your email and signing you in.
forgotPw.title: Forgot your password?
forgotPw.subtitle: Enter your email address and we’ll send you a link to reset your password.
forgotPw.sentBody: If an account with that email exists, we sent a reset link. It expires in 1 hour.
legal.notice.body: By continuing, you agree to our {terms} and {privacy}.
resetPw.missingToken: This reset link is missing a token. Please request a new one.
resetPw.tooShort: Password must be at least 6 characters.
resetPw.title: Choose a new password
resetPw.subtitle: Your new password must be at least 6 characters.
join.problem.used: This invite has already been used
format.league.title: League (round robin)
format.league.tagline: Everyone plays everyone; the table decides.
format.league.bestFor: Season-long divisions and social groups where everyone wants a full card of matches.
format.league.tradeoff: The fairest ranking, but the most matches — n(n−1)/2 fixtures for one leg.
format.league.body.0: Every entrant plays every other entrant a fixed number of times (one leg, two legs, or more). Wins and results feed a points table; the table's final order is the result.
format.league.body.1: Nobody is eliminated, so it maximises play for everyone — the trade is time: eight entrants in one leg is 28 matches. Ties in the table resolve by your tiebreaker cascade (head-to-head, difference, and so on).
format.groups-knockout.title: Groups + knockout
format.groups-knockout.tagline: Pools qualify their best into a bracket — the World Cup shape.
format.groups-knockout.bestFor: One-day tournaments: guaranteed group matches for all, a dramatic bracket for the best.
format.groups-knockout.tradeoff: Great balance of fairness and drama; needs group sizes planned around your court time.
format.groups-knockout.body.0: Entrants split into pools that each play a round robin. The top of each pool — top 2 is classic — cross over into a knockout bracket: A1 meets B2, B1 meets A2, so group winners are rewarded with the easier semi.
format.groups-knockout.body.1: Everyone gets their group matches even on a losing day, and the finish is winner-takes-all. This is the default shape for most one-day multi-court events.
format.knockout.title: Knockout (single elimination)
format.knockout.bestFor: Big fields on tight time — a 32-draw resolves in five rounds.
format.knockout.tradeoff: Fastest to a champion, but half your entrants play exactly one match.
format.knockout.body.0: A seeded bracket: winners advance, losers are done. Byes fill the bracket when the entry count isn't a power of two, protecting the top seeds.
format.knockout.body.1: It's the most time-efficient format there is, and the least forgiving — an early upset sends a favourite home after one match. Pair it with a group stage if everyone deserves more play.
format.double_elim.title: Double elimination
format.double_elim.tagline: Everyone gets a second life in the losers bracket.
format.double_elim.bestFor: Competitive brackets where one bad match shouldn't end a tournament.
format.double_elim.tradeoff: Roughly double the matches of a knockout; the grand final can need two games.
format.double_elim.body.0: Two brackets run side by side: lose in the winners bracket and you drop to the losers bracket, where a second defeat is final. The survivors of each meet in the grand final — and a losers-bracket champion must beat the winners-bracket champion twice.
format.double_elim.body.1: Beloved in esports, TT and pool: fairer than single elimination, still bracket-shaped, at about twice the match count.
format.swiss.title: Swiss
format.swiss.tagline: Equals play equals for a fixed number of rounds.
format.swiss.bestFor: Big fields, short time, nobody eliminated — chess's gift to weekend events.
format.swiss.tradeoff: Everyone plays every round and the ranking converges fast; pairings only exist round by round.
format.swiss.body.0: Round 1 is seeded. From round 2, entrants meet opponents on the same score — winners play winners, strugglers play strugglers. After a fixed number of rounds (5 covers up to 32 entrants well), the table decides.
format.swiss.body.1: Nobody is knocked out and blowout mismatches disappear after round one. The schedule can't be printed in advance — each round's pairings come from the live standings, which the app draws for you.
format.stepladder.title: Stepladder finals
format.stepladder.tagline: The lowest seed climbs; the top seed waits at the summit.
format.stepladder.bestFor: Finals night after a league — every match eliminates someone, seeding really matters.
format.stepladder.tradeoff: A brilliant finish for 3–5 qualifiers; the top seed plays only once.
format.stepladder.body.0: Seed 4 plays seed 3; the winner plays seed 2; that winner plays seed 1 in the final. Finishing top of the league buys you rest and a single decisive match — finishing fourth means winning three in a row.
format.stepladder.body.1: Usually staged as the finale after a league stage, turning a table into an event.
format.americano.title: Americano & Mexicano
format.americano.tagline: Individuals rotate partners; personal points decide.
format.americano.bestFor: Padel and social doubles — solo sign-ups, everyone plays with everyone.
format.americano.tradeoff: Maximum mixing and zero elimination; needs a points-based sport and even court usage.
format.americano.body.0: You enter alone. Each round the app deals new pairs — in Americano the rotation is fixed so everyone partners everyone; in Mexicano the pairings re-rank each round from live points (1st + 4th vs 2nd + 3rd), so games stay close.
format.americano.body.1: Every point you win is yours personally, whoever your partner was; the individual leaderboard decides. The dominant format in padel clubs, and a joy for any doubles sport's social night.
format.ladder.title: Ladder
format.ladder.tagline: A living ranking — challenge above you, defend below.
format.ladder.bestFor: Ongoing club rankings with no fixed schedule — squash boxes, TT clubs.
format.ladder.tradeoff: Runs forever with matches on demand; there's no printed fixture list by design.
format.ladder.body.0: Entrants sit on a ranked ladder. Anyone may challenge a player within range above them (three rungs is typical); win and you take their rung. Matches happen whenever the two of you play — the app records the result and reorders the ladder.
format.ladder.body.1: Perfect for a club season with no fixture nights: the ladder is always current, and the year-end order is its own trophy.
format.explainer.fullGuide: Full guide in the help centre →
schedule.notice.nothingToSchedule: Nothing to schedule — no free slots or nothing unscheduled.
schedule.notice.nothingNew: Nothing new to generate — fixtures are up to date.
schedule.notice.notReady: Stage is not ready to complete (undecided fixtures remain).
schedule.notice.divisionFinished: Stage completed — that was the last stage, the division is finished. 🏆
schedule.notice.advanced: Stage completed — top {n} advance; {gen} fixture(s) generated for the next stage.
schedule.notice.stageAdded: Stage added — {n} fixture(s) generated for the top {topN}.
schedule.notice.stageAddedLater: Stage added — fixtures will generate when the previous stage completes.
schedule.noStages: This division has no stages yet — recreate it with a format, or define stages via the API.
schedule.noFixtures.can: No fixtures yet — generate them when entrants are registered.
schedule.chip.timedTitle: This match has a kick-off time
settings.org.about.desc: Shown on your public organisation page — who you are, how to join, who to thank.
settings.org.tour.desc: Walk through the basics again — organisation, settings and creating a competition.
settings.org.brand.desc: Colors your public pages and TV noticeboard. Competitions can override it.
settings.org.logo.title: Organisation logo
settings.org.logo.desc1: Shown on public tournament pages and the navigation bar.
settings.org.logo.desc2: Click the square to upload (PNG, JPG, WebP — up to 2 MB).
settings.org.sponsors.tooBig: Sponsor logos can be up to 2 MB.
settings.org.sponsors.name: Sponsor name
reg.withdraw.body: Frees the spot — the oldest waitlisted entry moves up.
reg.withdraw.moneyLockPassed: The fee will not auto-refund — the refund lock has passed (refund manually if owed).
reg.withdraw.moneyRefunds: The paid fee refunds automatically (refund lock off).
reg.refund.bodyConfirmed: Money only — {name} stays confirmed and keeps the spot.
reg.remind.notSent: Reminder not sent — email isn't configured.
reg.stripeOffline.pre: Card payments are offline — registrants can't pay until Stripe is reconnected under
reg.stripeOffline.post: . The public page shows the division as temporarily unavailable.
reg.disputed: {n} payment(s) disputed — check your email and Stripe dashboard. Disputed rows are flagged below.
reg.heading: Registrations
reg.searchAria: Search registrations by reference or name
reg.settings.capacityHint: Capacity (blank = uncapped; overflow joins the waitlist)
reg.settings.feeNote: Fee changes apply to new sign-ups; current entries keep their price.
reg.settings.payOrganiserDesc: Cash or bank transfer. Entries are pending until you mark them paid.
reg.settings.payInstructionsPlaceholderO: Leave blank to use your organisation's instructions
reg.settings.payInstructionsPlaceholder: e.g. bank details or “pay cash on the day” — or set organisation-wide instructions in Settings → Payments
reg.settings.markdownNote: Markdown formatting works here; {reference} becomes the registrant's reference number.
reg.settings.cardPaymentDesc: Paid via Stripe when they register · confirmed automatically · unpaid entries expire after 48h and the waitlist moves up.
reg.settings.connectStripeFirst: Connect Stripe in Settings → Payments first
reg.settings.refundLock: Refund lock (withdrawals stop auto-refunding after this)
reg.settings.refundLockSet: After this moment withdrawals keep the fee — refunds become your call, per entry.
reg.settings.refundLockNone: No date set: every paid withdrawal auto-refunds in full, right up to match day.
reg.form.intro: Ask registrants anything beyond name, email and date of birth.
reg.form.examples: e.g. shirt size, dietary needs, emergency contact, club membership number, or a “I agree to the code of conduct” checkbox.
reg.form.label: Question label
reg.form.saveNote: Save settings to apply form changes.
reg.empty.none: No registrations yet. Share the public competition page — the Register button appears while a division is open.
reg.empty.noMatch: Nothing matches “{query}” — check the reference for typos (letters O/I are never used; try 0/1).
reg.chip.disputeLostTitle: The cardholder was repaid by Stripe and the amount was recovered from your Stripe balance.
reg.action.markPaidTitle: Record a cash/bank payment and confirm the entry
reg.action.remindTitle: Email the registrant a payment reminder
reg.action.refundTitle: Refund the remaining amount — the entry keeps its spot
reg.action.retryRefundTitle: The automatic refund failed — retry it
reg.action.evidenceTitle: Download the dispute evidence pack — receipt, activity log and fixtures, ready for the Stripe dispute response
reg.duplicateTitle: Same contact email as another active entry in this division — may be intentional (e.g. a parent entering two kids).
